data.title: What's Going On With Applied Digital Stock Monday?
data.description:
Applied Digital Corp. (NASDAQ:APLD) stock rose Monday, reflecting a strong performance as the broader market also shows positive momentum. The move comes as major indices like the S&P 500 and Nasdaq are up, indicating that APLD is moving with broader market trends.

The Catalyst

The stock is currently trading at $39.90, marking a daily change of 5.85%. This uptick aligns with a general bullish sentiment in the market, particularly within the technology sector, which is seeing gains of 0.69%.

In addition to the overall market strength, Applied Digital Corporation’s 12-month performance has been impressive, with shares increasing by 504.79%. This long-term growth reflects strong investor interest and confidence in the company’s business model.

Delta Forge 1: Scaling The AI Infrastructure

On Jan. 22, Applied Digital announced it had broken ground on Delta Forge 1, a 430-megawatt (MW) AI Factory campus located in the southern United States. According to the company, the facility is designed to support up to 300 MW of critical IT load, specifically engineered for the high-density cooling and power requirements of hyperscale AI workloads.

Technical Analysis

The stock is trading 25.1% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 42.2% above its 100-day SMA, demonstrating robust short-term and long-term strength. Shares are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, indicating a strong bullish trend.

The RSI is at 63.32, which is considered neutral, while the MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum. This combination suggests that while the stock is not overbought, there is still positive momentum driving the price higher.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock is performing well, traders should remain cautious of potential pullbacks.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on Apr. 13.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 17 cents (Down from Loss of 8 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $77.08 million (Up from $52.92 million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $29.88. Recent analyst moves include:

  • B. Riley Securities: Buy (Raises Target to $53.00) (Jan. 9)
  • Roth Capital: Buy (Raises Target to $58.00) (Jan. 8)
  • Needham: Buy (Maintains Target to $41.00) (Jan. 8)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Applied Digital, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 99.16) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Applied Digital’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, indicating that the stock is currently outperforming the market. Investors should consider this bullish trend while being mindful of potential volatility.

Price Action

APLD Price Action: Applied Digital shares were up 5.47% at $39.75 at the time of publication on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image by jackpress via Shutterstock

data.title: Novavax Stock Is Surging Monday: What's Driving The Action?
data.description:
Novavax Inc (NASDAQ:NVAX) shares are up on Monday following a recent licensing agreement with Pfizer Inc (NYSE:PFE) for its Matrix-M adjuvant, while renewed concerns around elevated respiratory illness levels are also lifting interest in vaccine makers. Here’s what investors need to know.

  • Novavax shares are climbing with conviction. What’s behind NVAX gains?

Pfizer’s Bold Bet On Novavax’s Technology

Under the non-exclusive agreement, Pfizer will utilize Novavax’s Matrix-M adjuvant in products across two disease areas, with Novavax receiving a $30 million upfront payment. Additionally, the agreement includes potential development and sales milestones totaling up to $500 million, along with tiered royalties on products incorporating Matrix-M.

The companies have not disclosed specific products or timelines related to this licensing deal, but it marks a significant collaboration for Novavax’s technology, enhancing its efforts to monetize its vaccine platform.

Why Pfizer's Deal Matters For Novavax Stock

For Novavax, the Pfizer licensing deal is strategically important because it validates Matrix-M as a best-in-class adjuvant and plugs the company into Pfizer's global commercial engine.

Instead of shouldering all development and marketing risk alone, Novavax can now collect non-dilutive cash through the $30 million upfront payment, up to $500 million in milestones and ongoing royalties on future product sales.

That revenue can help fund late-stage programs, strengthen the balance sheet and extend Novavax's cash runway without issuing more shares.

Just as importantly, having a blue-chip partner like Pfizer broadens physician and regulator familiarity with Matrix-M, which could make it easier for Novavax to win additional partnerships in respiratory, COVID-19 and other vaccine markets. It also diversifies revenue beyond Novavax's flagship vaccine.

Rising Respiratory Illnesses Add Another Tailwind for Novavax Stock

Beyond the Pfizer licensing deal, Novavax is also benefiting from renewed investor focus on the broader respiratory illness landscape. Recent data from the CDC show elevated flu and RSV activity across the U.S., with the 2025–26 flu season now classified as moderately severe, driving increased attention toward vaccine manufacturers

This backdrop is supportive for Novavax, which remains positioned in seasonal and respiratory vaccines as demand trends stay elevated longer than usual.

Higher awareness around flu, RSV and potential COVID upticks reinforces the relevance of Novavax's technology and commercial offerings at a time when investors are reassessing underappreciated vaccine platforms.

What Do Mixed Momentum Indicators Mean?

The stock is currently trading 25.4% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 25.5% above its 100-day SMA, indicating a strong short-term trend. Shares have increased by 4.56% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 64.40, which is considered neutral territory, while MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum. The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, leaving traders cautiously optimistic.

  • Key Resistance: $10.50

How Will Novavax Tackle Emerging Health Threats?

Novavax is a biotechnology company that develops vaccines. The company works in the clinical stage of development with a focus on delivering novel products that prevent a broad range of diseases. It works together with its wholly owned Swedish subsidiary to produce vaccine candidates to respond to both known and emerging disease threats.

The company believes its vaccine technology has the potential to be applied broadly to a wide variety of human infectious diseases. The company manages its business as one operating segment, the development and commercialization of vaccines.

Upcoming Earnings Report: What To Watch

Investors are looking ahead to the company’s next earnings report on Feb. 26.

EPS Estimate: Loss of 56 cents (Down from a loss of 51 cents YoY)

Revenue Estimate: $85.52 Million (Down from $88.31 Million YoY)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Novavax, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: 45.66 — Trading at a steep discount relative to peers.
  • Momentum: 60.87 — Stock is not currently outperforming the broader market.

The Verdict: Novavax’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a stock that is struggling to gain traction in a bearish market. While the Value score suggests it may be undervalued, the low Momentum score indicates that traders should be cautious in the current environment.

Top ETF Exposure

  • SPDR Series Trust SPDR S&P Biotech ETF (NYSE:XBI): 3.06% Weight

Significance: Because NVAX carries meaningful weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

NVAX Shares Surge Monday

NVAX Price Action: Novavax shares were up 5.67% at $9.69 at the time of publication on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: AdobeStock Image

data.title: Nvidia Unveils World's First Open Weather AI Software Stack
data.description:
Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ:NVDA) is opening up artificial intelligence (AI)-powered weather forecasting tools to help more organizations build faster, cheaper prediction systems without relying solely on supercomputers.

At the American Meteorological Society's Annual Meeting, Nvidia introduced a new Nvidia Earth-2 family of open models, libraries, and frameworks, calling it the world's first fully open, accelerated weather AI software stack.

The company said the tools can speed every stage of forecasting, from processing observations to producing 15-day global forecasts or local storm forecasts.

Key Product Capabilities

Nvidia said Earth-2 Medium Range, built on the Atlas architecture, predicts up to 15 days ahead across 70+ variables.

It also rolled out Earth-2 Nowcasting, powered by StormScope, which uses generative AI trained on satellite and radar data to produce zero-to six-hour storm forecasts in minutes.

Nvidia also introduced Earth-2 Global Data Assimilation, powered by HealDA, to generate atmospheric "initial conditions" in seconds on GPUs instead of hours on supercomputers, and said pairing it with Medium Range creates the most skillful predictions from an open, all-AI pipeline.

Industry Adoption

Nvidia said developers and agencies are already running and testing Earth-2 tools, including Brightband, the Israel Meteorological Service, Taiwan's Central Weather Administration, The Weather Company, and the U.S. National Weather Service, plus energy and finance users such as TotalEnergies, Eni, GCL, Southwest Power Pool (with Hitachi), AXA, and S&P Global Energy.

Earth-2 Medium Range and Nowcasting are available through Nvidia Earth2Studio and on Hugging Face and GitHub, while Nvidia said Earth-2 Global Data Assimilation will launch later this year.

The push comes as others expand into AI weather forecasting.

Competitive Landscape

Last July, Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:PLTR) expanded its decision-making software into AI-powered weather forecasting through a new partnership with Tomorrow.io.

Palantir teamed up with the weather analytics firm to embed real-time atmospheric data and predictive weather tools directly into its platforms, aiming to improve operational readiness for defense, aviation, government, and infrastructure customers.

NVDA Price Action: Nvidia shares were down 0.43% at $186.86 at the time of publication on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by Chung-Hao Lee via Shutterstock

data.title: Nvidia Doubles Down On CoreWeave As Real-World AI Testbed
data.description:
The relationship between Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ:NVDA) and CoreWeave, Inc. (NASDAQ:CRWV) has far surpassed the traditional vendor-customer dynamic. 

Monday's $2 billion investment cements CoreWeave as Nvidia's go-to lab for next-generation infrastructure experiments.

  • CRWV stock is climbing. See the chart and price action here. 

While hyperscalers like AWS and Google increasingly move toward their own custom chips, CoreWeave has stayed Nvidia-native. 

CoreWeave serves as the tip of the spear for Nvidia's deployment. The new Rubin architecture and Vera standalone CPUs will roll off the line in 2026. CoreWeave will then stress-test them in highly specialized AI factories.

“CoreWeave’s deep AI factory expertise, platform software, and unmatched execution velocity are recognized across the industry. Together, we’re racing to meet extraordinary demand for NVIDIA AI factories—the foundation of the AI industrial revolution,” Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said. 

The Software Feedback Loop

The R&D collaboration extends deep into the software stack. Nvidia is actively validating CoreWeave's proprietary tools—specifically SUNK (Slurm on Kubernetes) and CoreWeave Mission Control—to be integrated into Nvidia's own reference architectures.

CoreWeave CEO Michael Intrator spoke directly to the synergistic relationship with Nvidia in a statement announcing the latest investment. 

“From the very beginning, our collaboration has been guided by a simple conviction: AI succeeds when software, infrastructure, and operations are designed together,” Intrator said. 

CoreWeave's Lab Status

CoreWeave's "lab” status gives Nvidia a controlled environment to refine its full-stack offerings—networking, storage and compute—without the friction of a competitor’s proprietary hardware. 

By the time a new Nvidia chip reaches the broader market, CoreWeave has already written the instruction manual for running it at peak efficiency.

“AI is entering its next frontier and driving the largest infrastructure buildout in human history,” Huang said. 

CoreWeave clusters are running at the cutting edge of what Nvidia's hardware can do. The deepening partnership positions CoreWeave at the forefront of the AI revolution buildout 

CRWV Price Action: CoreWeave shares were up 8.73% at $101.19 at the time of publication Monday, according to Benzinga Pro. 

Image: Shutterstock
